note: SNG-089 (Erik-directed): some traditions OPEN a craft to anyone, because a world with more of that craft in it is the world they want. Housed at the Quiet House in the Crossing. Living treaty — it reacts to world arcs.
principle: An Accord craft is FREELY ACCESSED, never free to learn. **You still spend the skill point.** You are simply not gated by origin, region, teacher, or ring-distance penalty. **The tuition is the JOURNEY** — you must go, through the waygate, and be taught.
housedAt: the_quiet_house (The Crossing) — the one roof under which no people may raise a hand. It has never been violated. **The price of an accord may be your people's word that they never will.**

waygates.note: Accord Waygates run from the AXIS GATE to each signatory's homeland, so an open craft is REACHABLE and not merely permitted.
waygates.builtBy: the Numinous (nobody else could have; they will not say why and do not use them)

living: ⛔ THE ACCORDS REACT TO WORLD ARCS. A signatory may SUSPEND its accord against a people that has wronged it. If the Marchers [violence] march, the Stillhold [peace] may close its Calm Word to them — and that closure is a world-event, dated, propagating, and felt. A standing rule is a footnote; **a living treaty is a story.**

signatories.why: They want more people able to stand between harm and the innocent. That IS the goal. **Hoarding it would be the betrayal.**
Fewer deaths. Life should propagate — including through hands that are not theirs.
Every war not started is their doctrine proved.
They repair compulsively. Teaching it means more things get fixed, and that is simply better.
Fewer beams fall on fewer people.
They cannot bear watching people waste what they have.
More making in the world. Obviously.

refusals.because: An ending must be TENDED, not handed out. A careless death-craft is an atrocity. **They refuse on ethical grounds and they are right.**
The Harborward's entire point is that nobody knows. **A taught secret is a spent secret.**
A taught lie is a dead lie.
'We teach our own. Violence given away is violence multiplied.'
Every bargain is personal. **A taught bargain is no bargain.**
Reading a mind unbidden is a violence. They know it. They do it anyway. **They will not arm you to.**
It takes a life to learn. It cannot be given.
